title: ProcScript Labels
text: ProcScript Labels A label allows you to name a line of ProcScript. This line can then be branched to with a goto statement. The format for a label is: ProcLabel : where ProcLabel is the literal name of the label, which can be a maximum of 128 characters. Note: Your ProcScript will be more structured if you avoid using goto statements and use the repeat and while statements instead. Example: goto ProcScript Label The following example deletes the first 1001 records, then exits the current component. This is typically used when a component is defined for transaction processing. operation exec $1 = 0 retrieve start: ; the label ends with a colon (:) if ($1 = 1001) store exit else remocc $1 = $1 + 1 endif goto start ; but no colon is used in the goto line end; exec Copy Was this topic helpful?
